message:
fix(ad): symmetric subgradient tie-averaging in Sort VJP

The Sort VJP previously used a simple inverse-permutation to route
gradients back to original positions, which is incorrect when input
values are tied — the result depended on arbitrary sort-stability
ordering. Replace with a two-pointer group scan that identifies
contiguous runs of equal values (including NaN-as-own-bucket via
partial_cmp().is_none()) and averages upstream gradients across each
tied group, implementing the canonical symmetric subgradient.

Also add two defensive guards before VJP computation:
- axis >= rank returns AdError::EvalFailed (prevents index panic)
- g_vals.len() != total returns AdError::EvalFailed (shape mismatch)

New tests:
- sort_vjp_descending_unscrambles_gradient: basic 3-element no-tie case
- sort_vjp_averages_tied_gradients: [2,1,1] with gradient [3,9,6]
  verifies tied 1s receive (3+9)/2 = 6.0 each
- sort_vjp_axis0_unscrambles_each_column: 2D tensor sorted along axis 0

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
